title: Eriotheca roseorum
what_description: Eriotheca roseorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae, native to Central America. It is a climbing shrub with woody stems, bearing ovate to elliptic leaves that are 10-20 cm long and 5-10 cm wide, with a cordate base and a rounded apex. The leaves are dark green on the upper surface and pale green on the lower surface, with a prominent midrib and lateral veins. The plant produces showy, funnel-shaped flowers that are 5-7 cm in diameter, with five white petals and a yellow center, containing numerous stamens and a prominent pistil. The fruit is a capsule, containing numerous seeds, and is a characteristic feature of the Malvaceae family.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Eriotheca roseorum are inflammation and fever, which are reduced by the anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties present in the plant. Diabetes is controlled through the stimulation of insulin secretion by Eriotheca roseorum's flavonoids. Diarrhea is alleviated due to the plant's ability to reduce intestinal inflammation and increase the absorption of water and electrolytes. Additionally, hypertension is regulated through the plant's vasodilatory effects, while fungal infections are treated by the inhibition of fungal growth. The plant also exhibits antimicrobial properties, helping to heal wounds, treat bacterial infections, and alleviate gastrointestinal issues such as digestive problems and asthma symptoms, as well as reducing pain and inflammation associated with rheumatism, arthritis, and skin infections, in addition to promoting wound healing, and finally, it is also used to treat insect bites and burns.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Eriotheca roseorum are attributed to its antioxidant activity, which helps protect the body against oxidative stress and cell damage. The bioactive compound flavonoids, present in the plant, are responsible for its antimicrobial properties, exhibiting inhibitory effects against various microorganisms, thereby preventing infections. The anti-inflammatory properties of Eriotheca roseorum, mediated by saponins and phenolic acids, help alleviate swelling and pain in affected areas. The analgesic effects of the plant, resulting from the action of alkaloids, provide relief from pain and discomfort, while the antifungal properties, attributed to terpenes, inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens, preventing infections and diseases. The presence of these bioactive compounds contributes to the medicinal value of Eriotheca roseorum, making it a potential source of natural therapeutic agents.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Eriotheca roseorum are flavonoids, which are responsible for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, while quercetin, a major component of flavonoids, exhibits antiviral and anti-cancer activities by inhibiting the proliferation of cancer cells and reducing inflammation. Phenolic acids, particularly hydroxybenzoic and hydroxycinnamic acids, have been identified as key contributors to the plant's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, kaempferol, a flavonoid glycoside, has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, anti-cancer, and cardiovascular-protective properties due to its ability to scavenge free radicals and inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. The plant's asterosaponins, a type of triterpenoid saponin, have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and modulating the immune response. These healing constituents work synergistically to provide Eriotheca roseorum with its medicinal properties.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Eriotheca roseorum are infusion, decoction, tincture, salve, and tea. Infusion is prepared by steeping the leaves in hot water to treat fever and digestive issues. Decoction involves boiling the roots and stems to treat respiratory problems and skin conditions. Tincture is made by soaking the leaves in a solvent to treat pain and inflammation. Salve and tea preparations are used to treat wound healing and as a soothing agent, respectively.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Eriotheca roseorum improperly include inducing nausea, causing diarrhea, and inducing vomiting due to its toxic principles such as cardenolides and bufadienolides, which can cause gastrointestinal irritation and stimulate the vomiting reflex when ingested in large amounts. These toxic compounds can also cause nausea by irritating the stomach lining and intestinal tract, leading to feelings of queasiness and discomfort. Additionally, improper use of Eriotheca roseorum can lead to diarrhea, a symptom of gastrointestinal upset caused by the plant's toxic principles. The bufadienolides in Eriotheca roseorum can also interfere with the normal functioning of the heart, leading to cardiac arrhythmias and other cardiac complications. In severe cases, improper use of Eriotheca roseorum can lead to life-threatening complications, making it essential to handle the plant with caution and follow proper guidelines for use.

It belongs to the subfamily Apocynoideae, which includes a range of species with similar characteristics and growth habits. The genus Eriotheca is a relatively small group of flowering plants, with Eriotheca roseorum being one of the species within this group. Eriotheca roseorum is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Eriotheca roseorum reflects its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants in the Apocynaceae family.

intro: Eriotheca roseorum, commonly known as rose orchid tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Eriotheca roseorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Eriotheca roseorum is often made into teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional remedies such as poultices and salves.
